title: Sequential delighting translator + from-scratch trunk: canonicalize the pixels first, localize second
hypothesis: Backbone is off-limits this round (mnv3s_features8.pt / mobilenet_v3_small banned), and the last eight rounds (36-43) are jointly refuted or never-scored while sharing one unexamined structural choice: whichever objective targets lighting/appearance invariance (contrastive pretraining in 41/42, a pixel-reconstruction auxiliary in 43) was always bolted onto the SAME feature map that also has to serve localization -- one trunk, one descriptor, two competing gradients fighting over the same bottleneck. Exp 42's contrastive version measurably lost (846.42 m vs champion 743.07 m). Exp 43's reconstruction version was never actually refuted -- it crashed silently on berlin and prignitz (both train logs are 0 bytes, no epoch ever printed) while frankfurt and munich trained cleanly to loss ~5.48-5.83 and exported normal-sized ONNX files (runs/20260722_214356_iter3/train_frankfurt.log, train_munich.log); loop.sh launches all four areas as concurrent background processes sharing one GPU (autoresearch/loop.sh:337-347), so this reads as resource contention from the added reconstruction decoder at 4-way parallel launch, not a refutation of reconstruction-as-a-signal. Rather than resubmit that same architecture and gamble on the same crash, this experiment tests a mechanistically different bet inspired by library L1's flavor A (the flying pre-model, never yet tried -- only flavor B, the training-only auxiliary, was attempted in exp 43): split canonicalization and localization into two SEQUENTIAL stages with separate losses instead of one shared trunk with a blended gradient. A tiny translator network runs first and is graded ONLY on repainting the crop to look like the clean daytime reference (pixel-aligned supervision the frozen relight pipeline already provides for free, per library L1); the from-scratch localization trunk runs second, entirely on the translator's output, and its features are never directly graded against pixel fidelity -- only against location. This removes the shared-bottleneck tug-of-war exp 43 hypothesized (untested) by construction: the translator can spend 100% of its capacity on appearance cleanup and the trunk can spend 100% of its capacity on discarding-lighting/keeping-layout, because neither one's loss reaches into the other's own feature map directly (only indirectly, through the ordinary chain rule of stacking them).
method: In model/model.py: delete the ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk entirely (import, weights path, loader function) -- no torchvision constructor, no checkpoint file anywhere in model/. Add a new from-scratch DelightTranslator module: two stride-2 conv down-blocks (3->24->48 channels) into one 3x3 bottleneck conv (48 channels) into two stride-2 ConvTranspose2d up-blocks (48->24->3), whose output is added to the ORIGINAL input as a residual delta and clamped to [0,1] -- predicting a correction rather than a full repaint keeps the identity mapping trivial at init and biases the network away from a blurry mean-image collapse, since most of the output pixel value still comes straight from the input. Replace the deleted pretrained trunk with a from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->64 channels, stride 16, 8x8 output grid) -- the same shape already proven trainable in exps 41-43 -- but it now reads the translator's canonicalized output, not raw pixels. Delete the dark-expert head and its gate (model.py's self.dark_logits/self.gate from exp 12): with an explicit upstream canonicalization stage, a downstream day/night blend is redundant machinery duplicating the same job. The C4 rotation stack now wraps translator+trunk jointly: each of the 4 pixel-rotated raw copies passes through the translator individually, then through the trunk, then the single unified field head, then the same C4 logit-average and sharpened soft-argmax decode as before. In model/train.py: sample_epoch also extracts, for every sampled crop, the pixel-aligned clean-reference crop from the untouched reference.tif raster at the SAME (cx, cy, angle) already used for the lighting-bucket crop (reference.tif is already opened this way in compute_cell_weights, so this is the same file, a new read path) -- this is the translator's training target, free per library L1. The training loss becomes loss_fn(...) [unchanged CE + coord L2 + conf BCE] + 1.0 * L1(translator_output, clean_reference_crop), both terms active every step from step one, no separate pretraining phase. Optimizer collapses to a single param group / single LR (e.g. 1e-3) for all parameters, since everything is from-scratch and there is no pretrained-feature-preservation reason for a 10x trunk/head LR split anymore.
expected_outcome: If decoupling canonicalization from localization into two separately-lossed sequential stages is the missing ingredient (vs. exp 41/42/43's shared-bottleneck versions), worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 580-700 m (6-22%), with the clearest mechanism-level signature being the translator's own L1 loss converging to a visibly low value (< 0.05 on [0,1] pixels) by the end of training AND the metric beating exp 42's 846.42 m by a wide enough margin to distinguish 'decoupling helps' from 'from-scratch trunk alone doesn't matter'. Falsification forks: (a) if the translate L1 loss stays high/flat (> 0.08), the tiny translator is too small or the residual-delta design collapsed toward near-identity output, and this run is mechanically equivalent to exp 42's plain from-scratch trunk with no invariance help -- expect a result near 800-850+ m, redirecting toward a slightly larger translator or abandoning the flying-translator family for the training-only auxiliary (retried with a smaller decoder so it doesn't repeat exp 43's resource crash) rather than concluding sequential-vs-joint objective placement doesn't matter; (b) if translate loss is low but the primary metric doesn't improve over 743.07 m, canonicalization quality is fine but the trunk still can't rank confusable cells -- the wall is descriptor/ranking capacity independent of lighting invariance, redirecting the next iteration at the trunk's own capacity or the field's discretization rather than any further relighting attempt; (c) if the metric regresses sharply while translate loss is low, the residual delta likely oversmoothed genuine layout detail (blurring building edges along with lighting) chasing pixel fidelity -- the next iteration should weight the L1 term lower or restrict it to a coarser scale (e.g. downsampled target) so it grades tone/gain correction without penalizing sharp edges.
eli5: Instead of asking one part of the network to both 'clean up a murky night photo' and 'figure out where it was taken' at the same time, we split those into two separate steps done by two separate small networks. First, a tiny built-in filter looks at the raw camera frame and tries to repaint it to look like a clear daytime photo of that same spot -- it gets to see the real daytime photo as an answer key during training, which our simulator can hand out for free. Only after that cleanup does the place-guessing part of the network look at the picture, so it never has to fight through fog, glare, or night-time grain itself -- it just reads an already-brightened photo. Because the cleanup filter is graded purely on how good its repaint looks (not on whether it also happens to help with geolocation), and the place-guesser is graded purely on getting the location right (not on repainting anything), neither job has to compromise for the other's sake the way earlier attempts (which mashed both into one shared network) may have.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the RAW crop (before any cleanup) is copied into its four 90° turns; all four travel through the new cleanup filter and the place-guesser side by side for the inference-time vote -- same C4 mechanism as before, but it now feeds the new cleanup stage first instead of feeding the place-guesser directly

stages.name: Delighting translator
stages.detail: NEW stage: a tiny from-scratch filter (two shrink steps, one middle step, two grow steps) looks at the raw crop and outputs a correction that, added back onto the original pixels, is meant to look like a clear daytime photo of the same spot regardless of what lighting condition the input came from; trained by directly comparing its output to the real daytime photo of that exact spot (free answer key from the frozen simulator), and it flies on the drone too -- this cleanup happens every time, not just during training

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: a from-scratch, plain 4-stage conv stack (32->64->96->64 channels, no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3 family, no checkpoint file) -- unlike prior rounds, it now reads the CLEANED-UP photo from the translator above, never the raw pixels, and its own features are graded only on getting the location right, never on repainting anything

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: same recipe as before -- a 1x1 conv squeezes the 8x8 feature grid into a spatial code, kept alongside the texture average -- resized for the new 64-channel trunk

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: REMOVED: the old day/night switch and its second texture-only scorer are deleted. The cleanup filter now does the invariance job explicitly and upstream, so a downstream router blending two experts is redundant

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: a single unified scorer (no day/night blend) reads the layout summary directly into one heat map over the area

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the same contrast-sharpened balance-point decode as before, now reading the single unified heat map

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads the heat map's own shape as before (crisp hotspot vs. washed-out smear) -- same mechanism, now computed on the new descriptor

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the existing confusability-weighted place sampler (exp 35) feeds the usual epochs unchanged; NEW: every crop drawn, in every lighting bucket, also loads its pixel-aligned twin from the untouched reference.tif raster (same cx/cy/angle) as the translator's repaint target -- free, since the frozen relight pipeline renders every bucket from that same reference scene

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the usual grading (CE against the true spot, coordinate L2, confidence BCE) PLUS a new pixel-reconstruction L1 loss (weight 1.0) between the FLYING translator's own output and the true clean daytime crop -- active every step of the whole run; unlike exp 43, this loss grades the translator's own output directly and never touches the localization trunk's feature map, so the two jobs don't share a graded bottleneck

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: no separate pretraining phase -- translator and trunk train jointly end to end from step one; because everything is from-scratch, all parameters (translator + trunk + heads) share one LR group instead of the old 10x trunk/head split reserved for gently fine-tuning pretrained features

implementation_brief:
Edit model/model.py and model/train.py only. PRESERVE EXACTLY: train.py's CLI (--area --out-dir --data-dir --epochs --max-crops-per-bucket --seed), the ONNX export contract in model.py's docstring (one ONNX per area, 1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1] input, [[u, v, conf]] output, opset 17, export_onnx() signature unchanged), the C4 rotation helper (_c4_stack, byte-identical transpose/flip ops), the sharpened soft-argmax decode (DECODE_BETA=3.0, GRID_K=32, _grid_centers), loss_fn's existing CE+coord-L2+conf-BCE terms (add to them, don't replace them), calibrate_conf_shift/MIN_KEEP_RATE/SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD, and train.py's confusability-weighted sampler (compute_cell_weights, CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA) untouched in its own logic.

model/model.py: (1) Delete `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`, `IMAGENET_STD`, `_build_pretrained_trunk()`, and the `norm_mean`/`norm_std` buffers -- no torchvision model constructor and no checkpoint filename may remain anywhere in executable code (autoresearch/backbonecheck.py scans code with docstrings/comments stripped; old experiment history in the module docstring can stay as prose, but no NEW code path may reference a .pt/.pth file or a torchvision backbone name). (2) Add `class DelightTranslator(nn.Module)`: enc1 = Conv2d(3,24,3,stride=2,padding=1); enc2 = Conv2d(24,48,3,stride=2,padding=1); bottleneck = Conv2d(48,48,3,padding=1); dec1 = ConvTranspose2d(48,24,4,stride=2,padding=1); dec2 = ConvTranspose2d(24,3,4,stride=2,padding=1); forward: h=relu(enc1(x)); h=relu(enc2(h)); h=relu(bottleneck(h)); h=relu(dec1(h)); delta=dec2(h); return (x+delta).clamp(0,1). (3) Add a from-scratch trunk builder replacing `_build_pretrained_trunk`: a plain 4-stage conv stack, each stage Conv2d(stride=2)+BatchNorm2d+ReLU, channels 3->32->64->96->64, 128x128 in -> 64x8x8 out (stride 16, matching the grid the head already expects) -- this exact shape already trains successfully in this repo's history (exps 41-43). (4) In TinyLocNet.__init__: add `self.translator = DelightTranslator()`; build `self.features` from the new from-scratch stack (feat_ch=64); DELETE `self.dark_logits` and `self.gate`; keep a single `self.loc_logits = nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch*fmap_hw*fmap_hw, grid_k*grid_k)`; keep `self.conf_head` as-is (just re-sized for the new feat_ch). (5) In forward(x, return_logits=False, return_translated=False): x4 = _c4_stack(x); canon4 = self.translator(x4); fmap = self.features(canon4) [no ImageNet normalization -- input is already [0,1], translator output is also [0,1]]; f4 = fmap.mean((2,3)); layout4 = self.layout_squeeze(fmap).flatten(1); logits4 = self.loc_logits(cat([f4, layout4], dim=1)) [no gate/blend]; logits = logits4.reshape(4,n,-1).mean(0); rest of decode/confidence unchanged in shape. When return_translated=True, also return canon4.reshape(4,n,3,128,128)[0] (the un-rotated view's canonicalized image) so the training loop can grade it against the pixel-aligned reference crop.

model/train.py: (1) sample_epoch: open reference.tif once per area (rasterio, same pattern as compute_cell_weights) and for every sampled crop also call extract_crop on the reference array at the SAME (cx, cy, angle) used for the lighting-bucket crop, building a parallel `x_clean` uint8 tensor aligned 1:1 with `x`. Return (x, y, x_clean) from sample_epoch. (2) train_area's inner batch loop: `out, logits, translated = model(xb, return_logits=True, return_translated=True)`; `clean_b = x_clean[idx].to(device).permute(0,3,1,2).contiguous().float().div_(255.0)`; `translate_loss = torch.nn.functional.l1_loss(translated, clean_b)`; `loss = loss_fn(out, logits, yb) + 1.0 * translate_loss`. (3) Replace the trunk/head 10x-LR param-group split with a single group: `opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)`. (4) Update the `info` dict's `"init"` string to describe the from-scratch translator+trunk setup (no mobilenet/mnv3s mention) and add a logged field for the final epoch's mean translate_loss for lineage. Keep everything else (EPOCH_MULT, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS, cosine schedule, calibration) unchanged.

Caution (infra, not architecture): loop.sh trains all four areas as concurrent background processes on one GPU; exp 43's added reconstruction decoder is suspected to have caused a resource-contention crash on 2 of 4 areas (train_berlin.log/train_prignitz.log were 0 bytes, no epoch ever printed, while frankfurt/munich trained fine). Keep the translator as small as specified above (~50k params) rather than scaling it up, to avoid repeating that failure mode.
